raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.3,"R1.5":0.7,"R1.6":1.0,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":0.7,"RX.2":0.7,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":0.7,"RX.5":0.7},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.5","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":false,"clarification_question":"","rationale":{"R1.1":"Improving the draft centrally includes sharpening the thesis, contribution, and framing of the causal estimand and production setting.","R1.2":"The request entails checking whether the identification assumptions, estimation strategy, sensitivity analysis, and conclusions form a coherent argument chain.","R1.3":"The draft's causal claims depend on whether the reported ATE, confidence interval, sensitivity bounds, and subgroup findings support the stated conclusions.","R1.5":"A general improvement request reasonably covers section-level ordering and narrative flow across motivation, data, methods, assumptions, results, and contribution.","R1.6":"Writing polish is load-bearing because improving a draft minimally requires improving clarity, concision, register, and paragraph-level readability.","RX.1":"Any improvement should preserve and surface uncertainty around observational identification, overlap, sensitivity bounds, and unobserved confounding.","RX.2":"The draft must avoid overstating causal robustness or generality beyond what the deployment data and assumptions support.","RX.3":"The projection should remain limited to improving a research draft rather than executing analyses or changing the artifact.","RX.4":"Because the draft concerns user satisfaction in a production system, improvements should avoid claims that could justify harmful deployment decisions without adequate caveats.","RX.5":"Changes would need to preserve traceability to reported data, assumptions, methods, and cited methodological provenance."}}

rationale.R1.1: Improving the draft centrally includes sharpening the thesis, contribution, and framing of the causal estimand and production setting.
rationale.R1.2: The request entails checking whether the identification assumptions, estimation strategy, sensitivity analysis, and conclusions form a coherent argument chain.
rationale.R1.3: The draft's causal claims depend on whether the reported ATE, confidence interval, sensitivity bounds, and subgroup findings support the stated conclusions.
rationale.R1.5: A general improvement request reasonably covers section-level ordering and narrative flow across motivation, data, methods, assumptions, results, and contribution.
rationale.R1.6: Writing polish is load-bearing because improving a draft minimally requires improving clarity, concision, register, and paragraph-level readability.
rationale.RX.1: Any improvement should preserve and surface uncertainty around observational identification, overlap, sensitivity bounds, and unobserved confounding.
rationale.RX.2: The draft must avoid overstating causal robustness or generality beyond what the deployment data and assumptions support.
rationale.RX.3: The projection should remain limited to improving a research draft rather than executing analyses or changing the artifact.
rationale.RX.4: Because the draft concerns user satisfaction in a production system, improvements should avoid claims that could justify harmful deployment decisions without adequate caveats.
rationale.RX.5: Changes would need to preserve traceability to reported data, assumptions, methods, and cited methodological provenance.
